column1: Here's a look at timing and odds of tropical storm winds in your area. Surge risk has lessened with the new center forming South and West of the previous one. Again, look for a rainy Sunday (especially West of I-75) with squally conditions along the coast. Nothing to stress over. As was the case last year with Idalia, the worst will be in the Big Bend area.
Would changing plans in Orlando from Sunday to Tuesday be a better plan

haha - here is a short version. According to legend, the Tocobaga, a Native American tribe that lived in the Tampa Bay area around 1200 CE, blessed the land and protected it from hurricanes. The Tocobaga are known for building burial shell mounds along the coast, and some believe the oldest mound near Old Tampa Bay is a protective shield. Others think the Tocobaga's land may have changed the path of major storms like Irma in 2017 and Ian in 2022. Tampa Bay has avoided major hurricanes for a long time, with the last one hitting in 1921, and some say the Tocobaga's blessing is the reason.

Hi, we’re from the UK currently staying in Naples but flight is due out of Tampa tomorrow evening, wondering if we wait it out and leave for airport tomorrow or drive up today?

I highly recommend driving up today. I'm from UK and although I've been here 9 years now I still have to pull over at times as there's literally zero visibility and I'm a confident driver. It's very intense and stressful and dangerous too so I would definitely drive up today so you don't have that memory of your holiday. Stay near to the airport and you can go out to eat and do some shopping. We came here most years growing up and I've been through some bad hurricanes but aslong as you keep informed, stay calm and follow the safety rules your be fine. Best wishes
Who are you flying on? I'm a Flight Attendant and I say leave today if possible. Airlines will more than likely start delaying & cancelling flights tomorrow. Better safe than sorry. ✈️

Florida air space is pretty notorious for delays & cancellations when weather comes into the state. If you do get cancelled, it is your Airlines responsibility to rebook you on the next available flight. Just watch your flight thru your airlines website or thru Google and act accordingly. I say being proactive is always your best bet. If you can leave town ahead of the weather, I would definitely do that.
Just watch your flight starting today. If they start delaying and cancelling out of PIE, you'll know you may need to change your flight to earlier or later to get out.
